vulnerabilityAlerts.description: CVE-driven bumps bypass the Monday-only window so security fixes land ASAP. osvVulnerabilityAlerts adds OSV-database scanning for the version-pinned Docker images (GitHub-native Dependabot doesn't cover Docker), closing the one dependency axis without a 'silence != up-to-date' backstop. Requires repo Dependabot *alerts* (not PRs) enabled so Renovate can read the advisory feed.

lockFileMaintenance.description: pyproject.toml uses unbounded ranges (ansible-core >=2.18 etc.), so Renovate sees nothing to bump while uv.lock keeps exact pins frozen forever — silence ≠ up-to-date. A weekly lock refresh PR rides the same CI gate. NO automerge: >= ranges mean a refresh can contain major jumps (ansible-core 3.x one day); those deserve a deliberate look.

customManagers.description: Docker image tags inside Ansible docker-compose Jinja templates (containers/ is generated, so the source of truth is the .j2)

Pinned hook revisions in prek.toml. Renovate's built-in pre-commit manager only scans .pre-commit-config.yaml, not prek.toml, so these (pre-commit-hooks, ansible-lint, gitleaks) would age silently. Match each GitHub repo URL with its adjacent rev tag.

Ansible Galaxy collection pins in ansible/requirements.yml. The built-in ansible-galaxy manager isn't reliably matching this path here, so pin them explicitly — otherwise the collections age silently (silence != up-to-date, the same failure mode as the lockFileMaintenance note). Each name/version pair maps to the galaxy-collection datasource.

shellcheck-py pin in prek.toml, split out from the general prek.toml manager above: shellcheck-py/shellcheck-py publishes ZERO GitHub releases (tags only, 4-component vX.Y.Z.W), so under the github-releases datasource its rev could NEVER resolve an update and aged silently while the pypi-tracked pyproject.toml twin pin kept moving (2026-07-02 review M5). github-tags + loose versioning tracks + orders the v-prefixed 4-component tags; the packageRule below disables the dead github-releases duplicate and groups this with the pyproject pin so both bump in ONE PR (a lockstep test asserts they match).
Pinned prek version in the CI workflow (`pip install prek==X`). The pin keeps the hook toolchain reproducible; track it on PyPI so the pin doesn't age silently.

CrowdSec bouncer Traefik plugin pin in traefik.yml.j2 (Traefik static config). None of the other custom managers match this file, so this WAF-enforcement plugin would age silently — a stale version can lag Traefik API changes or miss plugin security fixes. depName is read from the moduleName line; track GitHub releases.

SOPS binary pin in sops_setup/tasks/main.yml. SOPS is the root of the secret-decryption trust chain and is the one version pin no other manager covers, so it would age silently (silence != up-to-date, same failure mode as the galaxy/crowdsec pins). getsops/sops publishes real GitHub releases, so github-releases is the right datasource. The paired per-arch sops_sha256 can't be auto-computed by a regex manager, so no automerge (packageRule below) — Renovate opens the version-bump PR, then finish the sha256 from the release's sops-<ver>.checksums.txt.

CI test-runner Python pin (`python-version:` input in ci.yml). helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests tracks the setup-python ACTION digest but not its version INPUT, so this was one of the two last silently-aging pins in the repo. A bump PR runs the whole test suite ON the new interpreter, which is exactly the compatibility proof; keep it moving with the host toolchain rather than frozen at the requires-python floor.

CI Node pin (`node-version:` input in ci.yml) — the other silently-aging setup-* version input; it only feeds renovate-config-validator, so stakes are low, but the tracking doctrine applies uniformly. Majors (22 -> 24) stay manual per the default automerge rules.

packageRules.description: Watchtower owns mutable :latest tags — Renovate manages only version-pinned images, so leave latest alone

Bundle minor + patch image bumps into a single weekly PR to cut noise; majors stay separate so they get a deliberate look. Auto-merge once CI (prek + image-smoke) is green and the release has soaked 7 days (mirrors watchtower's COOLDOWN_DELAY — filters fresh upstream breakage); the host health gate is the post-merge safety net.

LinuxServer.io images tag as <upstream-version>-lsNN (e.g. 4.0.17.2952-ls312). Regex versioning both orders these correctly and rejects non-release tags (commit hashes like 438eee94-ls46, nightlies) as invalid, so they're never offered. Optional 4th version part -> build, OS suffix (ubu2404) -> compatibility, -lsNN -> revision.

Keep prek.toml hook bumps in their own weekly group, separate from the container-image group. They match the custom.regex manager, so the non-major auto-merge + 7-day soak rule above already applies; this only overrides the group name. Majors stay manual (no automerge rule matches them).

Kill the general prek.toml manager's github-releases duplicate of shellcheck-py (that repo publishes no releases — the dedicated github-tags manager above is the live tracker). Without this the dead lookup still runs and could one day double-report.

shellcheck-py is pinned TWICE by design — prek.toml rev (github-tags, hook env) and pyproject.toml `shellcheck-py==` (pypi, validate_shell_templates' uv env) — and the two must move in lockstep (scripts/test_renovate_managers.py asserts it). One group = one PR touching both files, so neither PR is ever blocked by the lockstep test seeing half an update. Overrides the prek-hooks group above for this package.

Meilisearch (karakeep's search index) changes its on-disk DB format between MINOR versions and refuses to boot on an old database; the dumpless-upgrade flag is still experimental with a documented corruption risk, so upgrades are manual (snapshot first, then flag or dump/restore). Own PR + no automerge — and kept out of the non-major group so it can't block that bundle's automerge.

influxdb (scrutiny's TSDB) is watchtower-tier — it stays on the pinned 2.9 line and watchtower patches within it (scrutiny/CLAUDE.md). It's the ONE watchtower-tier image on a non-`latest` mutable tag, so the '/^latest$/' disable above doesn't cover it and Renovate's docker manager would otherwise offer 2.10+/3.x (Renovate PR #8 already walked it 2.2->2.9 once), marching a stateful DB off its pinned major.minor. Disable Renovate here so the two update planes agree — watchtower owns it.

Ansible Galaxy collections (requirements.yml). The in-file comment requires bumping them in lockstep with ansible-core, so these need a deliberate look — own group, no automerge (overrides the custom.regex non-major automerge rule above).
packageRules.groupName: ansible galaxy collections (manual — lockstep with ansible-core)

packageRules.description: CrowdSec bouncer Traefik plugin — a WAF-enforcement component whose bump requires a deliberate traefik redeploy (CI can't prove the plugin loads). Own group, no automerge, so it overrides the custom.regex non-major automerge rule above and gets an operator review.
packageRules.groupName: crowdsec bouncer plugin (manual — needs traefik redeploy)

packageRules.description: Portainer server (portainer/portainer-ce, daniel-server, GitOps-auto-deployed) and the Pi agent (portainer/agent, daniel-pi, MANUAL deploy only) MUST run the same version — the agent API tracks the Portainer server it connects to (portainer-agent/CLAUDE.md). One group = one PR touching both, and NO automerge: the Pi has no GitOps path, so if this auto-merged the server would deploy ahead while the un-deployed Pi agent silently lags (broken environment, no alert — Pi-only changes phantom-succeed). Merge, then redeploy the Pi by hand: deploy.yml --tags portainer-agent -e target=daniel-pi. scripts/test_renovate_managers.py asserts the two tags stay in lockstep. Overrides the non-major automerge group.

SOPS binary (getsops/sops) — the secret-decryption root of trust; its paired per-arch sops_sha256 needs a manual bump from the release checksums, so NO automerge (same manual-finish class as the galaxy collections + crowdsec plugin). Own group, so it overrides the custom.regex non-major automerge rule above and gets a deliberate operator review + sha256 finish.
packageRules.groupName: sops binary (manual — finish the per-arch sha256 from checksums.txt)
